concerns.text: The plan cites 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/metinovadilet/satelite_landsat_NDWI_NDVI/...' as a primary source for NDVI/EVI. This dataset is explicitly labeled 'Landsat' and 'NDWI' (Normalized Difference Water Index), whereas the spec (FR-001) and research goal require 'Sentinel-2' data and 'NDVI/EVI' indices. Landsat (30m resolution, 16-day revisit) and Sentinel-2 (10m resolution, 5-day revisit) have different spectral characteristics and temporal resolutions. Using a Landsat/NDWI dataset to validate a Sentinel-2/NDVI pipeline is an inappropriate dataset match that will not verify the intended ingestion logic.

The plan cites 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/svenmeijboom/geospatially_enriched_ndvi/...' (Afghanistan, year=2000) as a 'Verified' dataset for validation. The project scope (FR-001) is strictly 2018-2023. A dataset from the year 2000 is temporally misaligned with the target research window and cannot serve as a valid benchmark for the 2018-2023 Sentinel-2 ingestion pipeline. This dataset is inappropriate for the stated research question.

The plan states 'No external API authentication beyond public access (Google Earth Engine...)' and 'Assumption: Google Earth Engine API is accessible without authentication'. While GEE has a public tier, the `earthengine-api` Python client requires explicit user authentication (`earthengine authenticate`) to access the API, even for public datasets, due to quota and identity tracking. The plan's assumption that no authentication is needed is factually incorrect for the proposed tooling, risking pipeline failure during the 'Data Ingestion' phase.
